key_items: Bitcoin faces a Wall Street test as AI's mega-IPO wave targets the same capital -- OpenAI S-1 filing signals massive capital reallocation away from crypto
Bitcoin's $10 billion liquidation wave reveals why the AI boom is hurting crypto -- Leverage unwinding and investor appetite shift toward AI equities creating forced selling
Bitcoin price slips toward $62K local lows as bear-market history keeps repeating -- Technical breakdown continues with copycat BTC price moves despite geopolitical optimism
reasoning: The convergence of AI capital competition (OpenAI IPO at $850B-$1T valuation), persistent technical weakness ($62K support failing), and ongoing leverage liquidations creates a near-term headwind for Bitcoin despite some institutional dip-buying. The $10B liquidation wave and futures open interest declines signal weakening conviction among leveraged traders, while macro risk-off sentiment positioning Bitcoin as a leading indicator of broader market stress rather than a safe haven.
risks: Failure to hold $62K support level could trigger cascade liquidations accelerating decline toward $58K-$60K production cost zone within 3-5 days.
